purpose: Species held out of LoRA training data so we can measure true style-transfer generalization. The LoRA never sees PASS images for these species during training; held-out renders are scored against the same prompts on (a) base z-image-turbo and (b) z-image-turbo + LoRA to produce a paired-delta report.
rationale: Spread of expected difficulty: 3 user-documented-gold species (rhino, peacock, elephant) + 1 user-documented-difficult species (snow-leopard). If LoRA helps the easy ones AND doesn't tank the hard one, we have real signal. If LoRA only helps species it directly memorized in training, this catches it.

held_out_species.user_verdict: v1 plumage ideal, v2 colors ideal (gold standard)
all v1/v2/v3 FAIL — 'all snow leopards mid in Madhubani art'
held_out_species.expected_difficulty: hard — acts as canary for LoRA hurting difficult species

eval_protocol.primary_metric: delta_composite_paired (LoRA - base, averaged per species, then averaged across species)
